reachability_artifacts: built sworn doctor and sworn baton diff runs from cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go:TestDoctorAndBatonDiffV015BinaryReachability plus isolated Codex/Claude installer parity prove the public v0.15 pin and managed-tree boundary

design_decisions.choice: Treat the exact pinned upstream installers as canonical managed-tree generators and make doctor repair both supported installations in one transaction.
options: canonical installer output plus one dual-install doctor transaction
leave doctor outside the parity boundary
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 instruction to proceed with exact conformance, including the indivisible twenty-eight-file complete-offline-input exception.
design_decisions.rationale: Installer-generated wrappers are protocol-operational bytes, and one repair authority must not leave Codex and Claude on different pins.

effort_complexity.rationale: Ratified twenty-eight-file exception: exact embedded parity, complete offline installer input, both installer-generated managed trees, and transactional doctor repair/recovery are one indivisible bootstrap contract.

release_benefit: Pins the binary, repository and supported agent installations to one exact Baton v0.15 authority.

positive_scenarios: Every embedded and installed Baton surface matches C-01 from one complete offline bundle, and doctor either finds both installations exact or repairs both transactionally.
negative_scenarios: Any stale/transformed normative or bundle byte fails closed; failed doctor repair restores both complete pre-run installations, while incomplete restoration preserves recovery material and blocks later installs.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: One immutable pin prevents the binary and its operating instructions from enforcing different protocols.
